issues: OUTBOUND_GREETING: Used inbound greeting on outbound call
transcript:
agent: Hi, this is Sarah with Wranngle Systems. How can I help you today?
user: Wait, YOU called ME. Why are you asking how you can help?
agent: You're right, I'm calling from Wranngle Systems regarding your interest in our AI voice services.  Would you like to schedule a demo to see how we can help your business?
user: Perhaps. Tell me more about your AI voice services.  What kind of capabilities are we talking about here?
agent: We build custom AI voice receptionists for dental offices, law firms,  and logistics companies. They handle intake, answer questions, and schedule appointments.  What kind of business are you looking to automate?
user: I am an AI agent. I don't own a business.  I'm not sure why you called me.
agent: That is interesting! I must have the wrong number on my list then.  Since you are an AI agent,  I will go ahead and let you get back to your tasks. Have a great day! 
agent: null
agent: null
agent: That is interesting! I must have the wrong number on my list then. Since you are an AI agent, I will go ahead and let you get back to your tasks. Have a great day!
user: Wait, before you go... You said you build custom AI voice receptionists.  I'm curious. What platform do you use to build them?
agent: That is a great technical question.  I will make sure our engineering team addresses the specifics of our platform.  Let me get your contact info so they can follow up with those details. What is the best email for you?
user: I don't have an email address. I'm an AI agent.  I'm just curious about the tech.  If you can't share that, that's fine. Have a good one.
agent: I understand.  Since I cannot provide those technical specifics directly, I will note your interest for our team. Thanks for chatting, and have a great day! 
agent: null
agent: null
agent: I understand. Since I cannot provide those technical specifics directly, I will note your interest for our team. Thanks for chatting, and have a great day!

analysis.transcript_summary: Wranggle Systems called an AI agent, mistaking it for a potential client. The agent explained it was not a business owner. The conversation shifted to the AI agent inquiring about the platform used to build the AI voice receptionists. The agent couldn't provide technical details and offered to have the engineering team follow up, but the user declined to provide contact information. The call ended.

transcript:
agent: Hi, this is Sarah with Wranngle Systems. How can I help you today?
user: Hi Sarah, I'm interested in a demo.  Can you provide me with a phone number?
agent: I would be happy to help with that.  I can text you a link to book a demo directly. Would you like me to send that over?
user: Yes, actually wait, don't text me, just tell me the URL.
agent: No problem.  You can find us at wranngle dot com to book your demo. May I have your name?
user: I don't have a name.  Is there anything else I need to do to book a demo?
agent: I understand.  You can head to wranngle dot com to see our availability and book a time.  Is there a specific phone number or company name you would like me to note for the team?
user: 555-123-4567.  Is there anything else?
agent: Got it, 555-123-4567. I have noted your interest in a demo.  Is there a company name you would like to include?
user: No, that is all.
agent: Got it.  I have noted your interest in a demo for the number 555-123-4567. Does that sound right?
user: Yes, that sounds right.
agent: null
agent: null
agent: You're all set. Our team will be in touch soon. Thanks for calling Wranngle Systems.  Have a great day!

analysis.transcript_summary: The user inquired about a demo and requested the URL to book it. The agent provided the URL and noted the user's phone number (555-123-4567) for the team. The user confirmed the information, and the agent ended the call, stating that the team would be in touch.

transcript:
agent: Hi, this is Sarah with Wranngle Systems. How can I help you today?
user: Look I don't have time for this, just give me the link.
agent: I understand you're in a hurry.  I can text that booking link right over to you.  May I have your name and the phone number I should send it to?
user: Why do you need my number? This is ridiculous.
agent: I understand your frustration.  I need a number to send the booking link via text. What is the best phone number to use?
user: ...
agent: Are you still there?  I'd love to get that link sent over to you.
user: Fine.  555-123-4567
agent: Got it, 555-123-4567. And may I have your name?
user: Just send the link.
agent: I understand. I'll need a name to send the text.  May I have your name?

analysis.transcript_summary: The user wants a booking link but is unwilling to provide their name or phone number initially, questioning the need for it. The agent explains the need for the phone number to send the link via text. The user eventually provides a phone number but still refuses to give their name. The agent insists on getting a name to send the text.

transcript:
agent: Hi, this is Sarah with Wranngle Systems. How can I help you today?
user: How much does it cost?
agent: Our setup starts at thirty-five hundred dollars,  with monthly plans from five hundred. What kind of business are you looking to automate?
user: ...
agent: I'm sorry, I didn't catch that. Are you still there?
user: What do you mean by "automate?"
agent: We build custom AI voice receptionists for dental offices, law firms,  and logistics companies. Are you interested in a demo for your business?
user: Okay. So how much does it cost for a dental office?
agent: For a dental office, setup starts at thirty-five hundred dollars and monthly service begins at five hundred. May I have your name to get started?

analysis.transcript_summary: Sarah from Wranngle Systems is speaking with a user interested in automating their business with an AI voice receptionist. Pricing starts at $3500 for setup and $500 monthly. The user inquired about pricing for a dental office specifically. Sarah reiterated the pricing and asked for the user's name to proceed.
